evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis chart clearly shows log-scale training loss falling from ~2.19 to ~0.21 on left axis (blue) and cosine LR schedule annealing to zero on right axis (red/dashed); color-coupled axis labels aid reading as briefed.
evidence.typography: Script uses no pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype setting; pdffonts confirms DejaVu Sans embedded as Type 3 (bitmap-outline) rather than Type 42 (TrueType), and the default DejaVu Sans family is used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blue (#1f77b4) for loss and red (#d62728) for LR are colorblind-distinguishable (differ in hue AND lightness); directional discipline noted in caption (axes measure different quantities); no misleading green/red directionality.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout used; 6.5×3.8 in figure with reasonable margins; no overlapping labels; y-range on loss axis tracks actual data range on log scale; LR axis starts at 0 with appropriate headroom.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content well; legend placed upper-right inside plot (may overlap data at later steps); caption explicitly warns axes are independent quantities (good). Minor: legend placement could overlap with high-loss region for different datasets.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF exists but pdffonts shows 'BMQQDV+DejaVuSans' as Type 3 (non-selectable, rasterized glyphs) — not real vector text; pdf.fonttype=42 was never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: All four required outputs present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (15 KB), figure.png (66 KB), caption.tex (470 B), gen_figure.py (1.5 KB); bonus figure.svg also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both axes with bold labels, states exact numeric range for loss (~2.19 to ~0.21) and LR (1×10⁻³ to 0), explains log scale, notes axis independence — covers the key finding with specific numbers.

overall_strengths: Scientific content is clear and directly answers the brief: dual-axis time-series with color-coupled axis labels, log-scale loss, and cosine LR schedule all present.
Caption is exemplary: bold axis labels, precise numeric claims, and explicit warning about independent axis scales reduce Reviewer 2 complaints.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 (or svg.fonttype=none) set anywhere in the script, resulting in Type 3 (non-editable, bitmap-outline) fonts in the PDF — a common journal rejection point.
Default DejaVu Sans font family used throughout; no explicit font selection or rcParams configuration for a publication-quality look.
